title: PROphylaxis for Venous ThromboEmbolism in Severe Traumatic Brain Injury (PROTEST)

skipped_criteria: Inclusion ii): GCS 9-12 patients require ICU admission, ICB on initial CT, AND mechanical ventilation or increased ICB on repeat CT — no schema fields for ICU admission, CT findings, or ventilation status.
Inclusion iii): First dose must be given within 3 calendar days of injury — temporal logic relative to injury time cannot be expressed with available fields.
Exclusion i): Known hypersensitivity to Dalteparin/LMWH/heparin/pork products — no allergy/hypersensitivity field in schema.
Exclusion ii): Known history of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia or positive in vitro platelet-aggregation test — no prior medical history or lab result fields in schema.
Exclusion iii): Known septic endocarditis — no prior medical history field in schema.
Exclusion iv): Uncontrollable active bleeding — no field for active bleeding status in schema.
Exclusion v): Known major blood clotting disorders — no coagulation disorder history field in schema.
Exclusion vi): Known acute gastroduodenal ulcer with active bleeding — no GI history field in schema.
Exclusion viii): Known diabetic or hemorrhagic retinopathy — no ophthalmologic history field in schema.
Exclusion ix): Anticipated inability to receive sequential compression device on at least one lower extremity — no field for extremity injury or SCD eligibility in schema.
Exclusion x): Confounding factor explaining poor GCS (e.g., drug toxicity, seizure) — no field for alternative GCS etiology in schema.
Exclusion xi): Known irreversible coagulopathies — no coagulopathy history field in schema.
Exclusion xiii): Patient weight <45 kg or >120 kg — no weight field in schema.
Exclusion xiv): Not expected to survive more than 48 hours from admission — no survival prognosis field in schema.
